# @attestto/did-pki-resolver

> npm resolver package for the did:pki DID method -- converts X.509 CA certificates into W3C DID Documents.

Part of the [Attestto Open](https://attestto.org) ecosystem.

## Install

```bash
npm install @attestto/did-pki-resolver
```

Requires Node >= 18.

## What it does

Given a `did:pki` identifier, returns a W3C DID Document containing the Certificate Authority's public keys (as JWK), trust chain position, and PKI metadata. Implements the [did:pki specification](https://github.com/Attestto-com/did-pki-spec).

```typescript
import { TrustRegistry, DidPkiResolver } from '@attestto/did-pki-resolver';

const registry = new TrustRegistry('/path/to/attestto-trust/countries');
registry.load();

const resolver = new DidPkiResolver(registry);
const result = resolver.resolve('did:pki:cr:sinpe:persona-fisica');
console.log(result.didDocument);
```

## Core API

- `TrustRegistry` -- Loads and indexes country CA manifests from the attestto-trust data store.
- `DidPkiResolver` -- Main resolver class. Takes a `TrustRegistry`, exposes `resolve(did)`.
- `parsePkiDid(did)` -- Parse and validate a did:pki identifier string.
- `normalizeSubjectDN(subject)` -- Convert X.509 Subject DN to DID path segments.
- `buildDidDocument(cert, parsed)` -- Construct a W3C DID Document from certificate data.

## Source layout

```
src/
  index.ts       -- Public exports
  parser.ts      -- Parse/validate did:pki identifiers (spec section 4 ABNF)
  normalize.ts   -- X.509 Subject DN -> DID path segments (spec section 7)
  registry.ts    -- Trust registry, indexes attestto-trust manifests
  document.ts    -- Build W3C DID Documents from cert data
  resolver.ts    -- Main resolver, ties parser + registry + document builder
  countries.ts   -- Country-specific metadata (hierarchy names, endEntityHints)
  types.ts       -- TypeScript interfaces
  server.ts      -- Optional HTTP server entry point
```

## Data source

Reads from [`attestto-trust`](https://github.com/Attestto-com/attestto-trust) -- PEM-encoded X.509 certificates with `manifest.json` metadata per country. Currently covers Costa Rica; extensible to any national PKI.

## Related packages

- [`did-pki-spec`](https://github.com/Attestto-com/did-pki-spec) -- The W3C DID method specification this implements
- [`attestto-did-resolver`](https://github.com/Attestto-com/attestto-did-resolver) -- Unified HTTP resolver service (wraps this package)
- [`attestto-trust`](https://github.com/Attestto-com/attestto-trust) -- PKI trust store data
